meta.title: EQ Test | What's Your Emotional Intelligence Score? - DopaBrain
meta.description: Measure your Emotional Intelligence (EQ) with 40 questions across 5 dimensions: self-awareness, self-regulation, motivation, empathy, and social skills.

dim.awarenessDesc: Recognizing your emotions and their impact
dim.motivationDesc: Inner drive and passion toward your goals
dim.empathyDesc: Understanding and caring for others' feelings

intro.desc: Discover your emotional intelligence across 5 dimensions with 40 questions and receive personalized advice.
intro.disclaimer: ⚠️ This test is for self-understanding only and is not an official psychological assessment.

q1: I can accurately identify the emotions I am feeling.
q2: When I'm angry, I can clearly understand why.
q3: I am aware of how my emotions influence my judgment and actions.
q4: I can honestly assess my own strengths and weaknesses.
q5: I have a clear understanding of my core values.
q6: When under stress, I can quickly identify the source.
q7: I use feedback from others to deepen my self-understanding.
q8: I frequently check in with my emotional state throughout the day.
q9: Even when angry, I avoid acting impulsively.
q10: I maintain composure in difficult or stressful situations.
q11: I recover quickly from negative emotions.
q12: I can delay immediate gratification to prioritize long-term goals.
q13: I can remain calm and rational even in conflict situations.
q14: I avoid excessive self-blame when I make mistakes.
q15: I strive to uphold my principles and values in any situation.
q16: My mood is generally stable without extreme highs and lows.
q17: I set goals and work persistently to achieve them.
q18: I do not give up easily when faced with obstacles.
q19: I have the resilience to bounce back after failure.
q20: I bring passion to my work even without external rewards.
q21: I constantly strive to improve and grow as a person.
q22: I enjoy setting challenging and ambitious goals.
q23: I am willing to make short-term sacrifices for long-term success.
q24: I genuinely enjoy learning new skills and knowledge.
q25: I can easily read the emotional state of others.
q26: I make an effort to understand situations from others' perspectives.
q27: I feel deeply moved when I see someone in pain or grief.
q28: I can sense how someone is feeling even without them saying it.
q29: I easily understand and respect people from different cultural backgrounds.
q30: I immediately notice when someone is feeling uncomfortable.
q31: I can look at problems from another person's point of view.
q32: In conflict situations, I consider the other person's feelings first.
q33: I can easily connect with new people.
q34: I am effective at mediating conflicts peacefully.
q35: I tend to bring out the best cooperation within a team.
q36: I listen attentively and respond thoughtfully in conversations.
q37: I am able to have a positive influence on the people around me.
q38: I consider building trust to be very important in relationships.
q39: I express myself clearly so others can easily understand.
q40: I feel comfortable in a wide variety of social situations.

grade.exceptionalDesc: Your emotional intelligence is outstanding. You masterfully handle your own and others' emotions with great leadership.
grade.excellentDesc: You have high emotional intelligence with strong interpersonal skills and excellent emotional regulation.
grade.goodDesc: Your emotional intelligence is solid. You handle emotions well in most situations with room for growth.
grade.averageDesc: Your emotional intelligence is at an average level. Focused practice in specific areas will help you grow quickly.
grade.developingDesc: You have great opportunities to develop your emotional intelligence. Consistent practice can lead to significant improvement.

desc.awareness: Self-awareness is the ability to accurately understand your own emotions, strengths, weaknesses, and needs. Higher scores here mean you make wiser, more informed decisions.
desc.regulation: Self-regulation is the ability to constructively control disruptive emotions and impulses. Higher scores mean you stay calm and clear-headed even under pressure.
desc.motivation: Motivation is the inner drive to achieve. Higher scores mean you persevere through adversity and consistently move toward your goals with passion.
desc.empathy: Empathy is the ability to understand and share the feelings of others. Higher scores mean you build deeper relationships and support others effectively.
desc.social: Social skills involve adeptly managing relationships and inspiring cooperation. Higher scores mean strong teamwork, leadership, and networking abilities.

advice.awareness: 💡 Keep an emotion journal. Spend 5 minutes a day noting what you felt and why. Mindfulness meditation is also highly effective for building self-awareness.
advice.regulation: 💡 Practice the 'stop and think' technique. When strong emotions arise, count to 10 and breathe deeply. Regular exercise and quality sleep are the foundation of emotional regulation.
advice.motivation: 💡 Break big goals into small milestones. Experiencing small wins daily strengthens your intrinsic motivation. Write down your 'why' and revisit it often.
advice.empathy: 💡 Practice listening without judgment when others speak. After conversations, ask yourself: 'How was the other person feeling?' Reading diverse fiction also effectively builds empathy.
advice.social: 💡 Try to have one genuine conversation with someone new each week. In conflicts, practice acknowledging the other person's perspective before expressing your own.

share.twitterText: My EQ score is {score} ({grade})! What's your Emotional Intelligence?
